command: PYTHONPATH=bindings/st-py python3 bindings/st-py/examples/zspace_inference_distortion_probe.py --prompt 'SpiralTorch is a Z-space runtime for language models because' --local-model <local-gpt2-dir> --activation-name-contains attn --activation-name-contains mlp --api-provider openai-responses --api-model gpt-4.1-mini --include-penalties --out runs/inference-distortion/local-gpt2-openai-probe.json

previews.local_baseline: SpiralTorch is a Z-space runtime for language models because it is a pure-Python library...
previews.local_distorted: SpiralTorch is a Z-space runtime for language models because it allows you to write your own code...
previews.api_text: SpiralTorch is a Z-space runtime for language models because it leverages the unique properties of Z-space inference distortion...

notes: The local HF path used the same distortion adapter for ZSpaceRepressionLogitsProcessor and activation intervention hooks.
The OpenAI Responses route accepted temperature/top_p but dropped frequency_penalty/presence_penalty through the SDK signature filter.
No API key, provider response id, or local absolute model path is stored in this sample.
